evidence_spans.span: The log-likelihood is obtained by taking the logarithm of the likelihood.
ℓ(θ) = log L(θ; x_{1:n}) = ∑_{i=1}^{n} log p(x_i ∣ θ).

results.rationale: The log-likelihood is defined as the logarithm of the likelihood function, and the formula ℓ(θ) = log L(θ; x_{1:n}) explicitly expresses the log-likelihood in terms of the likelihood function, establishing a FORMALIZES relation where the log-likelihood (source) formalizes the likelihood function (target).
